subject: stacker2 compression performance
body:
Hi all,

current K3 issues force us to record SPA data in uncompressed mrc files.
Obviously I want to compress them as fast as I can to save disk space.
The default 4 parallel stacks option in stacker2 can't keep up with 100
images/hour coming from the K3 server. Increasing this number to 20
doesn't seem to help much, indicated by transfer rates limited to <1GE
in a 10GE network, 3% CPU usage etc.

I would like to understand if this is bottlenecked by our
system/network/whatever or if stacker2 should be capable of processing
100 50-frame superres K3 movies (12k x 8k) on-the-fly.

relion_convert_to_tiff_mpi with 40 parallel processes performs faster,
but it would be a charm to do the on-the-fly compression stuff on the
same Windows system.

Thanks for your help and greetings from Jülich

Dan

--
Dr Daniel Mann

Forschungszentrum Jülich GmbH
Ernst-Ruska Centre for Microscopy and Spectroscopy with Electrons / ER-C-3 Structural Biology
Institute for Biological Information Processing / IBI-6 Cellular Structural Biology
Wilhem-Johnen-Straße, 52425 Jülich, Germany
Phone: +49 2461 61 9499
https://go.fzj.de/erc3-ibi6
Email: d.mann@fz-juelich.de
